steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence opens with a comma after constitution, signaling the start of an inserted phrase. The phrase according to legal researchers James Melton and Tom Ginsburg sits between the subject Iceland's constitution and the verb contains. An inserted phrase needs a comma on each side to mark both its opening and its close.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — researchers James Melton and Tom Ginsburg: no comma after Ginsburg, so the phrase is never closed. The verb contains runs directly into the inserted phrase with no boundary. ✗
  • Choice B — researchers, James Melton and Tom Ginsburg: adds a comma after researchers, which splits legal researchers from the names and creates a different, incorrect structure. Still no closing comma before contains. ✗
  • Choice C — researchers James Melton and Tom Ginsburg,: places a comma after Ginsburg, correctly closing the inserted phrase and setting up contains as the main verb. The full structure reads: Iceland's constitution, according to legal researchers James Melton and Tom Ginsburg, contains … ✓
  • Choice D — researchers, James Melton and Tom Ginsburg.: ends with a period, which would split the sentence into a fragment. ✗
That confirms C is correct.
